queries.question: For each occupation, list the salaries and salary differences as a percentage.
queries.sql: SELECT OccupationalStats.occupation, OccupationalStats.percentageGap, SalaryData.salary FROM OccupationalStats JOIN People ON OccupationalStats.occupation = People. occupation JOIN SalaryData ON SalaryData.personId = People.id
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics/prop#>
SELECT ?occ ?gap ?sal WHERE {
  ?os a :OccupationalStats ; p:occupation ?occ ; p:percentageGap ?gap .
  ?pe a :Person ; p:occupation ?occ2 .
  ?sd a :SalaryData ; p:hasPeople ?pe ; p:salary ?sal .
  FILTER(STR(?occ)=STR(?occ2))
}

queries.question: For each occupation, show me all the salaries and the salary differences both as the pay amount and a percentage.
queries.sql: SELECT OccupationalStats.occupation, OccupationalStats.percentageGap, differenceInSalaries, SalaryData.salary FROM OccupationalStats JOIN People ON OccupationalStats.occupation = People. occupation JOIN SalaryData ON SalaryData.personId = People.id
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics/prop#>
SELECT ?occ ?gap ?diff ?sal WHERE {
  ?os a :OccupationalStats ; p:occupation ?occ ; p:percentageGap ?gap ; p:differenceInSalaries ?diff .
  ?pe a :Person ; p:occupation ?occ2 .
  ?sd a :SalaryData ; p:hasPeople ?pe ; p:salary ?sal .
  FILTER(STR(?occ)=STR(?occ2))
}

queries.question: Show the pay gap percentage and job satisfaction of occupations, from lowest to highest satisfaction.
queries.sql: SELECT occupation, percentageGap, jobSatisfactionIndex FROM OccupationalStats ORDER BY jobSatisfactionIndex ASC
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?occupation ?percentagegap ?jobsatisfactionindex WHERE {
  ?occupationalstats a :OccupationalStats .
  ?occupationalstats p:occupation ?occupation .
  ?occupationalstats p:percentageGap ?percentagegap .
  ?occupationalstats p:jobSatisfactionIndex ?jobsatisfactionindex .
  ?occupationalstats p:jobSatisfactionIndex ?jobsatisfactionindex_o .
}
ORDER BY ASC(?jobsatisfactionindex_o)

queries.question: For each occupation, list the salaries and the pay gap as the pay amount.
queries.sql: SELECT OccupationalStats.occupation, OccupationalStats.differenceInSalaries, SalaryData.salary FROM OccupationalStats JOIN People ON OccupationalStats.occupation = People. occupation JOIN SalaryData ON SalaryData.personId = People.id
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics/prop#>
SELECT ?occ ?diff ?sal WHERE {
  ?os a :OccupationalStats ; p:occupation ?occ ; p:differenceInSalaries ?diff .
  ?pe a :Person ; p:occupation ?occ2 .
  ?sd a :SalaryData ; p:hasPeople ?pe ; p:salary ?sal .
  FILTER(STR(?occ)=STR(?occ2))
}

queries.question: Show the difference in salaries and job satisfaction of occupations, from lowest to highest satisfaction.
queries.sql: SELECT occupation, differenceInSalaries, jobSatisfactionIndex FROM OccupationalStats ORDER BY jobSatisfactionIndex ASC
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?occupation ?differenceinsalaries ?jobsatisfactionindex WHERE {
  ?occupationalstats a :OccupationalStats .
  ?occupationalstats p:occupation ?occupation .
  ?occupationalstats p:differenceInSalaries ?differenceinsalaries .
  ?occupationalstats p:jobSatisfactionIndex ?jobsatisfactionindex .
  ?occupationalstats p:jobSatisfactionIndex ?jobsatisfactionindex_o .
}
ORDER BY ASC(?jobsatisfactionindex_o)

queries.question: Show the pay gap percentage, difference in salaries and job satisfaction of occupations, from lowest to highest satisfaction.
queries.sql: SELECT occupation, percentageGap, differenceInSalaries, jobSatisfactionIndex FROM OccupationalStats ORDER BY jobSatisfactionIndex ASC
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/demographics/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?occupation ?percentagegap ?differenceinsalaries ?jobsatisfactionindex WHERE {
  ?occupationalstats a :OccupationalStats .
  ?occupationalstats p:occupation ?occupation .
  ?occupationalstats p:percentageGap ?percentagegap .
  ?occupationalstats p:differenceInSalaries ?differenceinsalaries .
  ?occupationalstats p:jobSatisfactionIndex ?jobsatisfactionindex .
  ?occupationalstats p:jobSatisfactionIndex ?jobsatisfactionindex_o .
}
ORDER BY ASC(?jobsatisfactionindex_o)

ambiguous_dsl.question: Working in different sectors, what will be the salary and pay gap?
What is the pay gap and job satisfaction across occupations, from lowest to highest satisfaction?
